questions.prompt: It's the middle of a stressful midterm week. Realistically, what does your side of the room look like, and how do you manage shared chores?

options.label: I always prioritize keeping things neat, even when stressed. I expect shared chores to be done on schedule.
I might leave clothes on my chair or books on my desk, but I never let trash or food pile up.
My personal space gets pretty messy, but I try to keep the shared areas clear out of respect.
I don't really notice clutter when I'm busy. Chores can wait until the weekend when I have time.
I'm totally unbothered by mess. We can clean whenever it gets unbearable.

questions.prompt: You need to sleep early for an important 8 AM class, but your roommate is a night owl who studies late with the desk light on. How do you want to handle this?

options.label: I need total darkness and silence to sleep. We'd need to agree on strict quiet hours and they should study elsewhere.
I can use a sleep mask or earplugs, but I'd ask them to keep the noise to a minimum.
I'm a heavy sleeper, so as long as they aren't having a loud conversation or blasting music, I don't mind.
I'm usually up late too, so it's rarely an issue. If it happens, I'd just ask them to turn off the main light.
I'd rather we just communicate on a day-to-day basis depending on what we both have going on.

questions.prompt: You're relaxing in the room on a Saturday afternoon, and your roommate brings two friends over to hang out without any prior warning. How do you react?

options.label: I'd be very frustrated. I expect advance notice and agreement before anyone comes into our shared space.
I might be slightly annoyed, but I'd put on headphones or leave to find a quieter spot.
I don't mind occasional unannounced guests during the day, as long as it's not during study time or late at night.
I love having people around! I'd probably stop what I'm doing and join the conversation.
It depends entirely on my mood that day; sometimes I'm fine with it, sometimes I need my space.

questions.prompt: You notice your roommate has been consistently using your expensive laundry detergent without asking. How do you address this?

options.label: I would immediately text them or leave a note asking them to stop using my things.
I would wait until we are both in the room and calmly tell them they need to buy their own detergent.
I would casually bring it up next time we're doing laundry together so it doesn't feel like a confrontation.
I probably wouldn't say anything to avoid awkwardness, but I'd start hiding the detergent.
I don't mind sharing at all! They can use whatever they need.

questions.prompt: Your roommate wants their friend from another college to sleep on your floor for the entire weekend. How do you feel about overnight guests?

options.label: I am strictly against having overnight guests in the room, regardless of the situation.
I'm only okay with it if it's a rare occasion and we discussed it at least a week in advance.
I'm fine with it for a night or two, as long as they let me know a day beforehand.
I'm very relaxed about it. They can have friends stay over whenever, as long as I have my bed.
I'd prefer if we host guests together and make it a fun weekend for all of us.
